rationale: All four cited ADRs are honored. ADR-0040: the spec correctly ratchets per-gate mixes/lenses/substitution from L4 (skill prose + operator memory) down to L2 (declared config), not to L1 — no dispatch, spawn, or gate-logic activation. internal/panel remains a CONFIG-FREE LEAF: `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep internal/config` is empty; panel imports no internal/config. ADR-0037: decision authority does NOT move — GateFacts (gate.go:70) carries no Gate field; the new decision-inert Panel.Gate is read only by the R7 advisory path (PanelGateAdvisoryDefault), never by PanelGateDecision/ApproveThreshold; the third-defense `threshold > 0` guard survives (gate.go:257); the spec-112 amendment note is present with §§3/6/8 untouched. ADR-0035: the unknown-`--gate` refusals (config.go:574 Load, :709 resolver, :59 --json-without-gate, cmd/config.go delegation) each emit a `recovery:` line enumerating all five valid keys, matching 109's established inline convention in the same file. ADR-0023 forward-only: 109's spec files are untouched by this branch; 112 ratchets on top. All touched packages (config/panel/complete/cmd) test green.
findings: ADR-0040 config-free leaf: `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep internal/config` returns non-zero/empty; no internal/config import in internal/panel/*.go. Correctly layered L4->L2 (declared config supplies creation-time defaults only; no L1 promotion).

ADR-0037 decision authority unmoved: GateFacts (internal/panel/gate.go:70) has no Gate field; Panel.Gate is read only by internal/complete's R7 advisory (PanelGateAdvisoryDefault at complete.go:390), not by PanelGateDecision. Third-defense guard `threshold > 0` present at gate.go:257. Spec-112 amendment (ADR-0037 lines 123-134) records the field as decision-inert metadata with §§3/6/8 explicitly unchanged.

ADR-0035 recovery lines: unknown-gate-key Load refusal (config.go:574) and gate-scoped resolver error (config.go:709) both enumerate all five keys (spec_approve, plan_approve, bead, final_review, adhoc) via strings.Join(PanelGateKeys); --json-without-gate (config.go:59) and the config show --gate delegation (config.go:432) carry recovery lines too. Verified by TestLoad_RefusesPerGateKnobs recoveryWants asserting all five keys.
ADR-0023 forward-only: `git diff 2f894401..d713a0b7 -- .mindspec/specs/109*` is empty — 109 was not re-opened; 112 extends via a separate branch.

Observation (non-blocking): config Load refusals use inline fmt.Errorf with a trailing `\nrecovery:` line rather than routing through internal/guard.FormatFailure. This matches the pre-existing 109 convention in the same file (config.go:493-509 predate 112) and config Load refusals are not internal/guard-style command guards; the recovery lines remain grep-able in ADR-0035's `recovery: <command>` form. Not a 112-introduced divergence.
